# Transcrição: 4. Not the Decision Maker

- **Fonte:** 4. Not the Decision Maker
- **Duração:** 02:33
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:33]

Hey, if you're lucky enough to be in a call and find out that the buyer, the person you're talking to, the prospect is not indeed a decision maker, you would ask them, hey, let me ask you, if half of what I'm saying is true, if half of what I'm saying is possible, are you the person to make a decision on this? And at this point you find out, no, I'm not. I'm not the decision maker. That person can be an influencer and that person is the only person you're talking to right now so don't let your attitude or your interest go down when you find out they're not the decision maker. Stay interested, okay? Treat them like they are. Hey, great, who would be involved in the decision of this magnitude? Who would we be talking to other than yourself? Who would be involved other than you because I know you're important to the organization, I know you're going to influence this in the right way? And oh, by the way, who's that person? What's that person's position? And hey, what do you think is important to them? See, at this point you almost have to start the call over. You were in a call with a person. You found out what was important to them. You found out that there was interest and you now have found out they're not the only decision maker or the ultimate decision maker. Now you're going to literally back up into your call and find out really what's important to them? What is that name? What do they, what bothers them? What are the two or three things I could solve for them? What's the single thing that if I told them I had a solution to that? They would be like, hey, get him over here and get him over here right now. See, look, the goal of the cold call is what? To get your product in front of that customer, to make a proposal, to present something. Your goal is not to talk to people. Your goal is not to warn people up. Your goal is not just to make calls. Your goal is to find out, do you have a product that can solve their problem? Set hooks, let them know that you are the solution to that problem and then find out who's the decision makers, if not you, who else? And by the way, when you're doing that, do not diminish the importance of the person that you're talking to. They will influence moving the ball down the field. And then find out what's important to those people, what's important to each of those people or there are other people that would be involved and then write their names down, collect that data, collect their positions and collect what they think this person thinks would be important to each and every one of those people. Look, in some of your sales, in some of your presentations, in some more complicated cycles with bigger companies, you can have seven, eight, nine, ten, eleven and twelve influencers for every one decision maker. Okay? Don't neglect this influencer and love this call.
